questions.question: In a passband ASK system operating over an AWGN channel, which factor most significantly degrades its performance at low SNR?

explanations.a: Incorrect! ASK is amplitude-modulated, so phase distortion has little effect.
explanations.b: Correct! Noise directly affects amplitude, making ASK highly sensitive at low SNR.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Bandwidth can be controlled with filtering and pulse shaping.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Detection type affects complexity, but noise dominates BER at low SNR.

questions.question: In coherent passband FSK systems, how is intersymbol interference (ISI) minimized?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Increasing frequency deviation alone does not guarantee orthogonality or ISI reduction.
explanations.b: Correct! Orthogonal tones (Δf = 1/Tb) ensure minimal overlap between symbols and eliminate ISI.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Raised cosine filtering is mainly for linear modulations like PSK to reduce ISI.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Differential encoding helps with phase ambiguity, not ISI.

questions.question: Compared to passband PSK, passband FSK is generally more robust in which type of communication channel?

answers.d: Flat-fading channels with strong phase jitter

explanations.a: Incorrect! PSK often performs better in pure AWGN with coherent detection.
explanations.b: Incorrect! FSK helps somewhat in frequency-selective channels, but not as significantly.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Narrowband impulsive noise affects both, but not the main advantage.
explanations.d: Correct! FSK relies on frequency, not phase, so it tolerates phase jitter better than PSK.

questions.question: Why is coherent detection preferred over non-coherent detection in passband PSK systems?

answers.b: It improves BER performance by recovering phase information.
answers.d: It makes the system more robust to frequency offset.

explanations.a: Incorrect! Coherent detection is more complex because it needs carrier synchronization.
explanations.b: Correct! Accurate phase recovery allows optimal decision boundaries and lower BER.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Bandwidth is mainly determined by symbol rate, not detection type.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Coherent detection is sensitive to frequency offset; it does not improve tolerance.

questions.question: What limits the minimum frequency separation in passband binary FSK for orthogonality?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Carrier phase does not affect frequency orthogonality.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Modulation index affects tone spacing indirectly, but orthogonality is set by symbol time.
explanations.c: Correct! Orthogonality requires Δf = 1/Tb, where Tb is the symbol duration.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Power affects SNR but not orthogonality.

questions.question: In passband ASK systems, what is the main reason it is rarely used in power-constrained wireless applications?

answers.b: It suffers from poor spectral efficiency.
answers.c: It has high peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR).
answers.d: It is highly susceptible to amplitude variations from fading.

explanations.a: Incorrect! ASK can be narrowband if filtered properly.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Spectral efficiency can be acceptable with pulse shaping.
explanations.c: Incorrect! PAPR is lower than multilevel schemes like M-ASK or QAM.
explanations.d: Correct! Fading affects amplitude directly, causing severe performance degradation.

questions.question: For Binary PSK in a passband system, what is the theoretical BER expression over an AWGN channel with coherent detection?

explanations.a: Correct! BER for coherent BPSK is Q(√(2Eb/N0)).
explanations.b: Incorrect! This corresponds to non-coherent or antipodal schemes differently scaled.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Equivalent to Q-function after scaling, but not standard Q-form.
explanations.d: Incorrect! This is a rough approximation, not exact BER.

questions.question: What is the impact of Doppler spread on passband PSK systems in mobile channels?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Symbol timing is only slightly affected; main effect is phase change.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Doppler affects phase as well as frequency, not only amplitude.
explanations.c: Correct! Rapid phase changes make carrier recovery difficult and increase BER.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Doppler usually degrades performance unless diversity or tracking is used.

questions.question: For coherent M-PSK, why does increasing M require higher SNR to maintain the same bit error rate?

answers.a: Because the angular separation between adjacent constellation points decreases

explanations.a: Correct! Smaller angular spacing makes symbols more susceptible to noise, requiring higher SNR to maintain BER.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Symbol duration may stay fixed; BER is affected by constellation spacing.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Bandwidth changes are secondary; BER is dominated by minimum distance.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Carrier frequency does not directly affect BER in ideal coherent M-PSK.

questions.question: In a passband PSK system over a fading channel, which factor primarily causes errors even if SNR is high?

answers.b: Rapid phase variations due to Doppler spread

explanations.a: Incorrect! At high SNR, AWGN has minimal effect.
explanations.b: Correct! Doppler-induced phase changes can rotate the constellation, causing errors even at high SNR.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Low symbol rate does not inherently increase errors.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Carrier frequency itself does not cause symbol errors unless combined with other impairments.
